reason: The statement does not apply because the text fragment describes the journey of Abram (later known as Abraham) and his family to the land of Canaan, which was their ancestral homeland. The text does not mention captivity or walking to an ancestral homeland they have never seen before. Additionally, the text specifically states that Abram was instructed by the LORD to leave his country and go to a land that the LORD will show him, indicating that it was not a return to an unknown ancestral homeland.
